Modi Naturals.summary: Management expects disciplined growth with improved operational efficiencies and premiumization across divisions, particularly benefiting from ethanol expansion and consumer brand momentum in FY27 and beyond.

guidance.statement: Top-line guidance of INR950 crores, factoring in ~50% capacity utilization of expanded ethanol capacity; potential to exceed if utilization improves.
Sustainable EBITDA margin guidance of 12-15% for ethanol division; current year margin benefited from grain price softening and value addition in byproducts.
Potential to cross INR1,100 crores with full capacity utilization of ethanol expansion.
Consumer division revenue growth expected to accelerate beyond FY26's ~2% growth, driven by expanded distribution and innovation.
Operational and financial benefits from expanded ethanol facility to become more visible from FY27 onwards as utilization levels improve and operations stabilize.
Expects to receive additional orders from private and PSU OMCs in the ongoing ESY cycle by end of June, beyond the INR400 crore order already received.
Planning value addition on alcohol side (portable/chemical) and byproducts; one innovation already implemented, second underway to be completed in a few months.
Consumer division EBITDA margin at ~8.3% in FY26; management aims to maintain profitability in new categories from year one or two.
Continued focus on improving working capital management and reducing inventory intensity across businesses.
Government may permit ethanol exports in the near future; policy developments expected in the next few weeks.
Policy push for CBG ecosystem is increasing; potential for separate business model but no immediate plans due to lack of synergy with current processes.
Evaluating opportunities in the food sector but nothing firmed up yet; will disclose if any material developments occur.

refused_to_guide: Ethanol export license requirements
Specific timeline for ethanol export approvals
Breakup of consumer division revenue by brand (Oleev vs. others)
Detailed capex plans for value-added ethanol products
Exact revenue contribution from new consumer categories (e.g., hing)

RBM Infracon.summary: Management expects strong growth in FY27 driven by order book execution, new contracts, and strategic investments in energy and semiconductor sectors.

guidance.statement: Target of ₹700 crores, with potential upside to ₹1,000 crores.
Profitability expected to increase due to higher-margin bids and improved execution.
Flawless, on-schedule delivery of current order book (over ₹700 crores as of H1FY27).
Maximizing utilization and returns on FY26 capital investments (₹113 crores gross block).
Incremental revenue building up with 5 new wells drilled and 70 older wells under O&M; production ramp-up over 2-3 years.
Infrastructure work to begin in FY27, with revenue expected from 2030; total project cost estimated at ₹2,700 crores.
Expecting additional packages from the 1.3 million Omani Riyals order with ACME CleanTech Energy.
Growing presence across South Asia and the Middle East, including bids for O&M platforms in Oman.
Expected final approval in 20-22 days, with migration to BSE imminent.
Commitment to provide quarterly results going forward.
Focus on repaying current debt before considering equity dilution for new projects.
Not planning offshore drilling at this stage due to high bid costs; will pursue via JV after 5 years.

refused_to_guide: Exact funding structure for the semiconductor project beyond the 30% stake and investor commitments
Specific timeline for equity dilution in the parent company for new projects
margin_drivers: Higher-margin bids in large projects (above ₹500 crores)
Execution discipline and scaling with margin protection
ONGC Nandej contract with USD 5.5 per barrel revenue model
Recurring revenue from ARCs with Nayara, Yara Fertilizers, RIL, and others
Geopolitical tailwinds (US dollar strength, crude oil price movements)

Hind.Oil Explor..summary: HOEC expects transformational growth over the next 3 years driven by drilling campaigns, infrastructure expansion, and production ramp-up across offshore and onshore assets.

guidance.statement: Targeting 10,000 to 11,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day by June 2027
Targeting 22,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day
Targeting 32,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day
PY-1: 12-15 million SCFs; B-80: 4,000-5,000 barrels; Assam: 45 million SCFs; Kharsang: doubling production
Drilling 2 wells in PY-1, 2 workovers in B-80, 3 new wells, 9 wells in Kharsang, and 3 wells in Assam (subject to pipeline availability)
Improving recovery factors across multiple assets through better reservoir management and infill drilling
Debottlenecking facilities, optimizing production profiles, and enhancing evacuation infrastructure
Funding growth through internal accruals and bank facilities with strict capital discipline
Focus on operational reliability, risk management, and cost optimization
Advancing FDP maturation, selecting development concept, and initiating FID
Potential to triple production to 45 million SCFs with pipeline infrastructure support
1P to 2P reserves valued at USD3 billion to USD5 billion depending on oil price

refused_to_guide: Debt and EBITDA outlook for FY27 and FY28

Wise Travel.summary: Management expects continued growth with expansion in fleet, international presence, and improved cash flows, though receivables and debt levels remain elevated.

guidance.statement: Plans to add at least 1,000 vehicles in the financial year.
Aiming to establish new overseas entities in the coming years, with Dubai as a key platform for growth.
Expects revenue growth of 30%-35% year-on-year in the coming years.
Anticipates continued positive cash flow generation, with most new businesses being cash-and-carry.
Considering dividends in the next 2-3 years as the company expands and stabilizes.
Receivables cycle expected to improve as new accounts are streamlined, reducing from 150-180 days to around 60 days.
Dubai business saw a 5%-10% drop in car rental due to geopolitical issues but expects recovery with new account signings.
Will continue to grow EV fleet (currently 400+ vehicles) despite current lack of economic sustainability, awaiting market acceptance.
Fuel price hikes are passed on to clients via escalation clauses in agreements when prices move beyond 5%.
Marginal improvement in EBITDA margins expected as utilization levels mature and operational efficiencies improve.
Expects improved profitability from FleetPro as utilization levels mature in the coming years.
Working on reducing receivables and improving cash flows, though debt levels are manageable and recoverable.

refused_to_guide: explicit revenue/EBITDA/PAT targets for FY27
detailed breakdown of revenue by vertical for FY27
specific debt reduction targets

P & G Health Ltd.summary: The company is poised for growth, driven by its Integrated Growth Strategy, and is focused on bringing superior healthcare solutions to consumers and patients.

guidance.statement: The company is expected to deliver high single-digit average sales growth.
The company is expected to deliver average profit growth of 13%.
The company's return on equity has increased by 42% over the past 5 years.

refused_to_guide: specifics of upcoming launches
forward-looking estimates of revenue growth

Gabion Technol..summary: Strong growth trajectory ahead driven by capacity expansion, order book visibility, and margin improvement.

guidance.statement: Targeting INR200 crores turnover, implying ~40%-45% growth over FY26.
Expecting turnover of more than INR300 crores.
Expecting 1-2 percentage points higher margin due to economies of scale and improved manufacturing margins.
Margin improvement driven by increased service business and advanced manufacturing technology reducing costs.
Total capex of ~INR8 crores (INR7 crores already spent, INR1 crore from IPO proceeds) to expand production capacity to 18,000 tons by September 2026.
Capacity to reach 18,000 tons by September 2026, enabling INR180 crores revenue from manufacturing alone.
INR200 crores order book to be completed within FY27, with 50% from projects and 50% from products.
Already booked INR44 crores in FY27 (as of May 2026) and expecting large orders to spill over to next financial year.
Current market share of 20%-25% in the Gabion industry, with plans to expand through specialized products and integrated solutions.
Expanding product portfolio with specialized offerings like 3D erosion control mats (sole manufacturer in India) to drive margins.
Expecting finance cost to remain around INR4.7 crores, similar to FY26.
Debt equity ratio expected to remain low at ~0.7.

refused_to_guide: Working capital days or steady-state working capital levels
Detailed breakdown of revenue growth drivers (e.g., government vs. private sector)
Specific capex plans beyond FY27
margin_drivers: Increased service business (higher margin than manufacturing)
Economies of scale from capacity expansion
Specialized products (e.g., 3D erosion control mats) driving margins

ROX Hi-Tech.summary: Management expects strong growth driven by AI, cybersecurity, and international expansion, with revenue guidance of INR500-750 crore by FY30 and EBITDA margins in the 15-17% range.

guidance.statement: 20% revenue growth expected for FY27
Revenue target of INR500 crore at 20% growth or INR700-750 crore with international expansion and M&A
EBITDA margins expected to revert to previous levels (15-17%) as supply chain normalizes
AI solutions already contributing 3-4% of revenue; expected to grow as AI adoption accelerates
NOC/SOC business to reach 15% of overall revenue with 100 customers in 3 years
International subsidiaries to drive growth alongside domestic operations
INR75 crore order book to be executed by September
Acquisitions in data center services (50% stake in INR8-10 crore revenue companies) and manpower services (USD 5-6 million revenue) to add volume
Inventory position expected to improve by September as supply chain normalizes
Improvement in working capital cycle from current levels
AI adoption by enterprises to drive demand for digital transformation and cybersecurity solutions
Revenue per employee expected to decline as company scales and adds fresh talent

Competitive data

India Pesticides.summary: Management expects sustained revenue growth driven by capacity expansion, backward integration, and new product launches, targeting INR 3,000 crores by FY31.

guidance.statement: Targeting INR 3,000 crores revenue by March 2031.
Revenue from Shalvis facility expected to be INR 70-80 crores in FY27, including contributions from two new molecules and second block.
PEDA/Pretilachlor revenue expected to be better than FY26, with full capacity utilization.
Projected EBITDA margin in the range of 18% to 20%.
Net working capital days expected to improve by 10-12 days from 223 days in FY26.
Capex plan for FY27 is INR 45 crores for India Pesticides and INR 90 crores for 100% subsidiary, primarily funded through internal accruals.
Capacity utilization for PEDA/Pretilachlor expected to be around 70%-75% due to seasonal demand.
Japanese partner forecasted to scale up by 30% in FY27, with discussions ongoing for one more molecule.
Hamirpur facility to provide significant pathway for future capacity expansion and specialty product manufacturing.
Commissioned intermediate plant for backward integration of herbicides to support supply chain stability and cost competitiveness.
Formulation capacity scaled up to 10,000 metric tonnes, enabling better market responsiveness.
Strong domestic demand for herbicides and intermediates expected to continue, supported by Make in India efforts.

refused_to_guide: Raw material pricing trends beyond current increases
Impact of El Niño on monsoon and agricultural output beyond general statements
margin_drivers: Price increases passed on to customers

Dhabriya Poly..summary: Management expects sustained revenue growth with expanding margins driven by new product launches and capacity additions.

guidance.statement: Targeting approximately 30% CAGR revenue growth over the long term.
Existing capacity can achieve more than INR450 crores of revenue for profile extrusion alone at 85% utilization.
20% plus EBITDA margin is sustainable in the longer run.
Improved profitability metrics are expected to be maintained.
INR100 crores strategic capex program to be deployed over FY26 to FY28.
Expecting around INR15 crores minimum contribution from WPC door and false ceiling wall paneling division in FY27.
Expecting around INR40 crores contribution from aluminum windows and facade division in FY27.
Strong order book of more than INR170 crores provides excellent revenue visibility.
Last year capacity utilization was 66% for PVC profile extrusion; targeting 85% utilization with existing capacity.
Raw material costs increased by 15%-18% due to geopolitical factors; prices have started cooling down.
Working capital cycle expected to improve materially during FY27 as inventories are consumed and supplier payment cycle normalizes.
Post INR100 crores capex implementation by FY28, initial asset turnover expected to be 2x.

refused_to_guide: merger of subsidiaries
brand consolidation strategy
margin_drivers: new product verticals (WPC doors, WPC wall/ceiling panels, aluminum windows/doors)

guidance.statement: Expect to grow 100-150 basis points higher than the market, implying double-digit growth
Chronic business expected to strengthen further, with internal target of improving share by at least 1% annually
High single-digit growth expected, with potential currency gains adding to margins
High teens growth expected in rest-of-world markets
Expected to be in the range of 20% to 21%, assuming stable geopolitical environment and cost clarity
Expected to remain between 4% to 5% of revenue
Expected to increase by 7% to 8% excluding Occlutech acquisition-related costs
Revised tax rate guidance of 27% to 29% (from previous 35% to 38%) due to transition to new tax regime
No major reduction in absolute dividend value expected despite higher tax rate; payout percentage may increase
Integration of Occlutech into MedTech business expected to take 12 months post-acquisition closure
No U.S. launch expected in FY27; potential out-licensing or later launch considered

INR 100+ crores achievable with current infrastructure
